options: A digital timer
B measuring cylinder
C ruler
D balance

questions.question: 10152025 speed m / s time / s How far does the object travel at constant speed? A 25 m B 50 m C 75 m D   125 m 3   A rock falls off a cliff onto a beach. The effect of air resistance on the rock is negligible. Which row describes the acceleration and speed of the rock as it falls? acceleration speed, A constant constant, B constant increasing, C increasing constant, D increasing increasing 3 4   Two rectangular blocks consist of different materials. Four different methods are suggested to compare the two masses. 1   Compare the accelerations with which they fall freely. 2   Compare the values of their lengths  breadths  heights. 3   Hang each in turn from the same spring. Compare the extensions. 4   Place one in the right-hand pan of a beam balance and the other in the left-hand pan. Which methods give a comparison of the two masses?, A 1, 2 and 3 B   1 and 2 only C   3 and 4 only D 4 only 5   A measuring cylinder containing 50 cm 3 of water is put on a balance. 150 g water 210 g object, A solid object is put in the cylinder and the water level rises to 75 cm 3 . What is the density of the object?, A 0.80 g / cm 3, B 2.4 g / cm 3, C 2.8 g / cm 3, D 8.4 g / cm 3 4
questions.text: 10152025 speed m / s time / s How far does the object travel at constant speed? A 25 m B 50 m C 75 m D   125 m 3   A rock falls off a cliff onto a beach. The effect of air resistance on the rock is negligible. Which row describes the acceleration and speed of the rock as it falls? acceleration speed, A constant constant, B constant increasing, C increasing constant, D increasing increasing 3 4   Two rectangular blocks consist of different materials. What is the density of the object?, A 0.80 g / cm 3, B 2.4 g / cm 3, C 2.8 g / cm 3, D 8.4 g / cm 3 4
options: A 25 m B 50 m C 75 m D   125 m 3   A rock falls off a cliff onto a beach. The effect of air resistance on the rock is negligible. Which row describes the acceleration and speed of the rock as it falls? acceleration speed
A constant constant
B constant increasing
C increasing constant

D increasing increasing 3 4   Two rectangular blocks consist of different materials. Four different methods are suggested to compare the two masses. 1   Compare the accelerations with which they fall freely. 2   Compare the values of their lengths  breadths  heights. 3   Hang each in turn from the same spring. Compare the extensions. 4   Place one in the right-hand pan of a beam balance and the other in the left-hand pan. Which methods give a comparison of the two masses?
A 1, 2 and 3 B   1 and 2 only C   3 and 4 only D 4 only 5   A measuring cylinder containing 50 cm 3 of water is put on a balance. 150 g water 210 g object
A solid object is put in the cylinder and the water level rises to 75 cm 3 . What is the density of the object?
A 0.80 g / cm 3
B 2.4 g / cm 3
C 2.8 g / cm 3
D 8.4 g / cm 3 4

questions.question: A cyclist is travelling in a straight line along a horizontal road at a constant speed. A constant driving force F acts on the cyclist in the forward direction shown. F Which statement about the magnitude of the frictional forces acting on the cyclist is correct?, A The magnitude is equal to F., B The magnitude is smaller than F, but greater than zero., C The magnitude is greater than F., D The magnitude is zero.
questions.text: A cyclist is travelling in a straight line along a horizontal road at a constant speed. A constant driving force F acts on the cyclist in the forward direction shown. F Which statement about the magnitude of the frictional forces acting on the cyclist is correct?, A The magnitude is equal to F., B The magnitude is smaller than F, but greater than zero., C The magnitude is greater than F., D The magnitude is zero.
options: A constant driving force F acts on the cyclist in the forward direction shown. F Which statement about the magnitude of the frictional forces acting on the cyclist is correct?
A The magnitude is equal to F.
B The magnitude is smaller than F, but greater than zero.
C The magnitude is greater than F.
D The magnitude is zero.

questions.question: A beam is pivoted at P. A force is applied to the beam. Which distance is multiplied by the force to give the moment of the force about P? A B C D P beam force 5
questions.text: A beam is pivoted at P. A force is applied to the beam. Which distance is multiplied by the force to give the moment of the force about P? A B C D P beam force 5

questions.question: The diagram shows an unbalanced rod. Two loads X and Y can be moved along the rod. pivot rod movement of rod movement of rod XY The rod turns in a clockwise direction, as shown. Which action could make the rod balance? A moving X to the left, B moving X to the right, C moving Y to the right, D moving the pivot to the left
questions.text: The diagram shows an unbalanced rod. Two loads X and Y can be moved along the rod. pivot rod movement of rod movement of rod XY The rod turns in a clockwise direction, as shown. Which action could make the rod balance? A moving X to the left, B moving X to the right, C moving Y to the right, D moving the pivot to the left
options: A moving X to the left
B moving X to the right
C moving Y to the right
D moving the pivot to the left

questions.question: It is important that an electric table lamp with a lamp shade does not get knocked over easily. shade base Which statement is correct? A The centre of mass must be as low as possible., B The lamp must have a shade which is heavier than the base., C The lamp must have a narrow base., D The lamp shade must be wide.
questions.text: It is important that an electric table lamp with a lamp shade does not get knocked over easily. shade base Which statement is correct? A The centre of mass must be as low as possible., B The lamp must have a shade which is heavier than the base., C The lamp must have a narrow base., D The lamp shade must be wide.
options: A The centre of mass must be as low as possible.
B The lamp must have a shade which is heavier than the base.
C The lamp must have a narrow base.
D The lamp shade must be wide.

questions.question: A bicycle braking system transfers energy from a kinetic energy store to an internal energy store. A motor converts energy from a chemical energy store (battery) to a kinetic energy store. What enables these energy transfers? braking system motor, A electrical work mechanical work, B electrical work electrical work, C mechanical work mechanical work, D mechanical work electrical work 6
questions.text: A bicycle braking system transfers energy from a kinetic energy store to an internal energy store. A motor converts energy from a chemical energy store (battery) to a kinetic energy store. What enables these energy transfers? braking system motor, A electrical work mechanical work, B electrical work electrical work, C mechanical work mechanical work, D mechanical work electrical work 6
options: A motor converts energy from a chemical energy store (battery) to a kinetic energy store. What enables these energy transfers? braking system motor
A electrical work mechanical work
B electrical work electrical work
C mechanical work mechanical work
D mechanical work electrical work 6

questions.question: A toy car is pushed a distance of 2.4 m with a force equal to 460 N. How much energy is transferred? A 190 J B 190 W C   1100 J D   1100 W
questions.text: A toy car is pushed a distance of 2.4 m with a force equal to 460 N. How much energy is transferred? A 190 J B 190 W C   1100 J D   1100 W

questions.question: The engine of a motor vehicle develops a large power. Which statement is correct? A The driving force acting on the vehicle must be large., B The engine must have a very large volume., C The engine must transfer large amounts of energy each second., D The vehicle must be very fast.
questions.text: The engine of a motor vehicle develops a large power. Which statement is correct? A The driving force acting on the vehicle must be large., B The engine must have a very large volume., C The engine must transfer large amounts of energy each second., D The vehicle must be very fast.
options: A The driving force acting on the vehicle must be large.
B The engine must have a very large volume.
C The engine must transfer large amounts of energy each second.
D The vehicle must be very fast.

questions.question: A solid cube has sides 0.50 m long and a mass of 120 kg. It stands on the ground on one face. Which pressure does the cube exert on the ground? A 480 kg / m 3, B 960 kg / m 3, C 4700 N / m 2, D 9400 N / m 2
questions.text: A solid cube has sides 0.50 m long and a mass of 120 kg. It stands on the ground on one face. Which pressure does the cube exert on the ground? A 480 kg / m 3, B 960 kg / m 3, C 4700 N / m 2, D 9400 N / m 2
options: A 480 kg / m 3
B 960 kg / m 3

questions.question: What  is  the  lowest  possible  temperature  (absolute  zero)  and  what  happens  to  the  energy  of particles at this temperature? lowest possible temperature / C particle energy A –273 particles have least kinetic energy, B –273 particles have zero gravitational potential energy, C 0 particles have least kinetic energy, D 0 particles have zero gravitational potential energy
questions.text: What  is  the  lowest  possible  temperature  (absolute  zero)  and  what  happens  to  the  energy  of particles at this temperature? lowest possible temperature / C particle energy A –273 particles have least kinetic energy, B –273 particles have zero gravitational potential energy, C 0 particles have least kinetic energy, D 0 particles have zero gravitational potential energy
options: A –273 particles have least kinetic energy
B –273 particles have zero gravitational potential energy
C 0 particles have least kinetic energy
D 0 particles have zero gravitational potential energy

questions.question: Which statement about the particles of a substance after condensation is correct? A They are close to each other and slide over each other., B They are close to each other and vibrate about fixed points., C They are far apart from each other and vibrate about fixed points., D They are far apart from each other and move freely within the container. 7
questions.text: Which statement about the particles of a substance after condensation is correct? A They are close to each other and slide over each other., B They are close to each other and vibrate about fixed points., C They are far apart from each other and vibrate about fixed points., D They are far apart from each other and move freely within the container. 7
options: A They are close to each other and slide over each other.
B They are close to each other and vibrate about fixed points.
C They are far apart from each other and vibrate about fixed points.
D They are far apart from each other and move freely within the container. 7

questions.question: The diagram shows a flask designed to reduce the loss of thermal energy from a hot liquid. plastic stopper vacuum hot liquid thin silvered walls of glass plastic supports Which methods of thermal energy transfer are the silvered walls designed to reduce? A conduction, convection and radiation, B conduction and convection only, C conduction only, D radiation only
questions.text: The diagram shows a flask designed to reduce the loss of thermal energy from a hot liquid. plastic stopper vacuum hot liquid thin silvered walls of glass plastic supports Which methods of thermal energy transfer are the silvered walls designed to reduce? A conduction, convection and radiation, B conduction and convection only, C conduction only, D radiation only
options: A conduction, convection and radiation
B conduction and convection only
C conduction only
D radiation only

questions.question: A drop of water from a tap falls onto the surface of some water of constant depth. view from above Water waves spread out on the surface of the water. Which statement is correct? A The waves are longitudinal and travel at the same speed in all directions., B The waves are longitudinal and travel more quickly in one direction than in others., C The waves are transverse and travel at the same speed in all directions., D The waves are transverse and travel more quickly in one direction than in others. 8
questions.text: A drop of water from a tap falls onto the surface of some water of constant depth. view from above Water waves spread out on the surface of the water. Which statement is correct? A The waves are longitudinal and travel at the same speed in all directions., B The waves are longitudinal and travel more quickly in one direction than in others., C The waves are transverse and travel at the same speed in all directions., D The waves are transverse and travel more quickly in one direction than in others. 8
options: A The waves are longitudinal and travel at the same speed in all directions.
B The waves are longitudinal and travel more quickly in one direction than in others.
C The waves are transverse and travel at the same speed in all directions.
D The waves are transverse and travel more quickly in one direction than in others. 8

questions.question: The optical image formed by a plane mirror is the same size as the object. Which row describes the other characteristics of the optical image formed? distance from the mirror real or virtual A same as object virtual, B smaller than object virtual, C larger than object real, D same as object real
questions.text: The optical image formed by a plane mirror is the same size as the object. options: A same as object virtual
B smaller than object virtual
C larger than object real
D same as object real

questions.question: The diagram shows a partly completed scale drawing of an upright object placed 3 cm in front of a thin converging lens of focal length 2 cm. FF What is the nature of the image formed by this lens? A diminished, inverted and closer to the lens than the object, B diminished, upright and further from the lens than the object, C enlarged, inverted and closer to the lens than the object, D enlarged, inverted and further from the lens than the object 9

C enlarged, inverted and closer to the lens than the object
D enlarged, inverted and further from the lens than the object 9

questions.question: A plastic rod and a dry cloth are uncharged. The  rod  is  now  rubbed  with  the  cloth  and  they  both  become  charged.  The  rod  becomes negatively charged because some charged particles move from the cloth to the rod. What is the charge on the cloth and which particles moved in the charging process? charge on cloth particles that moved A negative electrons, B negative neutrons, C positive electrons, D positive neutrons
questions.text: A plastic rod and a dry cloth are uncharged. The  rod  is  now  rubbed  with  the  cloth  and  they  both  become  charged.  The  rod  becomes negatively charged because some charged particles move from the cloth to the rod. options: A negative electrons
B negative neutrons
C positive electrons
D positive neutrons

AG V M Which circuit symbol is not present in the diagram?, A resistor, B voltmeter, C generator, D thermistor 11 28  The circuit shown contains three switches and four lamps P, Q, R and S. lamp P lamp Q lamp R lamp S switch 1 switch 3 switch 2 Which switches must be closed to light only lamps P and R?, A switch 1 only, B switch 1 and switch 2, C switch 1 and switch 3, D switch 2 and switch 3 29  An electric heater has a metal frame.
